essential_question: From flat polygons to solid shapes and the square roots that measure them — what's still solid in your memory?
objective: Revisit and connect the vocabulary and skills from Lessons 11–19 — polygon angles through rationalizing denominators — and surface any gaps before the Unit 02 capstone build and MUS Unit Test II.

agenda.title: Launch — Nine Lessons, Two Dimensions to Three

content.prompt: Name one lesson from 11–19 that was mostly flat (2D) and one that was solid (3D). Which was harder for you, and why?

content.heading: L11–L13 in sixty seconds each
bullets: L11 Polygons: Angles, Diagonals & Regular Polygons — interior/exterior angle formulas, diagonals, regular polygons.
L12 Circles: Parts & Vocabulary — radius, diameter, chord, arc, sector.
L13 Circumference & Area of Circles — circumference = 2πr, area = πr², what π actually is.

content.heading: L14–L16 in sixty seconds each
bullets: L14 Volume of Rectangular Solids & Cylinders — V = Bh (base area × height).
L15 Volume of Pyramids, Cones, Prisms & Spheres — the ⅓ and ⁴⁄₃ formulas, slant height.
L16 Surface Area of Solids — unfold the net, add up every face's area, lateral vs. total.

content.heading: L17–L19 in sixty seconds each
bullets: L17 Simplifying Radicals — pull out the biggest perfect-square factor.
L18 The Pythagorean Theorem — a² + b² = c², legs & hypotenuse, and its converse for testing right triangles.
L19 Rationalizing Denominators — multiply by the conjugate (or the radical itself) to clear the bottom.

content.heading: One from each third of the unit

content.preview: Next session: the Unit 02 capstone — a built/3D-printed solid with computed volume & surface area, verified by measurement.

success_criteria.text: I can name the key vocabulary and formulas from Lessons 11–19 (polygons through rationalizing denominators)
I can choose and apply the correct volume, surface area, or Pythagorean formula for a given solid or right triangle
I can identify which of Lessons 11–19 I need to reteach before the test

questions.prompt: The set of all points a fixed distance from a center point is a…
questions.explain: A circle is exactly the set of points at a fixed distance (the radius) from the center.

questions.prompt: A cylinder has radius 3 in and height 10 in. Find its volume in terms of π.
questions.explain: Cylinder volume is base area (πr²) times height.

questions.prompt: A right triangle has legs 6 and 8. What is the hypotenuse?
A rectangular box has a 4×5 base and height 6. Find its surface area.
questions.explain: Surface area is the sum of the areas of all six rectangular faces, each counted twice (top/bottom, front/back, left/right).

questions.prompt: Exit: which of Lessons 11–19 do you most need to review again before the capstone and the test — name it and why.
